A_intro:
vocational agriculture as outlined below.
The local school program and students are of paramount importance and should
receive first consideration.
Inherent in the whole plan for internship is the concept that the local school
system, Oregon State University, and the State Department of Education are all
vitally interested and concerned with the training of vocational agriculture teachers.
This undertaking should be a three-way cooperative partnership and recognize that
this close cooperation can do much to improve both theory and practice, bring them
closer together, as well as to coordinate and wify the efforts of the three agencies concerned toward the improvement of agriculture education.
Interns will be selected and placed cooperatively by the three participating
agencies. Final placement of interns will be at the discretion of the local school
A_methods:
district.
They will be placed in centers selected and approved jointly by the State Deyartment of Education and Oregon State University.
The period of internship shall be characterized by thorough supervision from
the master teacher in the local school system, from Oregon State University, and
from the State Department of Education.
Local school districts will employ the interns at a salary commensurate with
the salary schedule of the local school district, the intern's professional training,
the intern's actual teaching load, and the amount of academic credit concurrently
being given. The intern shall teach vocational agriculture one-half of the school
day and devote the other half to advanced study.
Provision must be made for supervision of farm projects.
-13-
Interns for the experiment will be selected from those students meeting the
A_results:
following qualifications:
Will have completed the college requirements of a B.S. degree in Agriculture.
The internship may be partially in lieu of, or it may be in addition to, conventional student teaching experience.
In all instances, particular care should be observed to insure that the participating agencies do not approve any prospective intern until such time as they are
reasonably well assured that the prospective intern has at least the minimum qualifications, experience, and education (both methods and subject matter) to enable
satisfactory performance when given responsibility for class.
The internship period will include both academic year and summertime experience
and be of at least six calendar months duration (preferably longer).
The State Department of Education will reimburse on interns' salaries in a
manner similar to that followed for fully certificated vocational agriculture instructors, providing that the policies outlined herein have been followed and conplied with, and providing further that a written memorandum of agreement accordingly
is properly consummated prior to the beginning of the internship.
A school system which is providing facilities for internships will incur expenses, some of which are properly chargeable to instruction and some of which are
properly chargeable to teacher training. The local school system should pay those
costs which are properly chargeable to instruction (with reimbursement through the
State Department of Education, Vocational Education Division, as outlined in the
preceding paragraph). The State Department of Education may approve additional reimbursement (on a 50 per cent basis) up to $125 for teacher training expenses.
Vocational agriculture instructors serving as master teachers shall have a
minimum of three years of successful teaching experience, and during the period of
internship shall be assigned at least two periods of vocational agriculture
classes, one period for project supervision, and one period for intern supervision.
A representative from the teacher training department at Oregon State University
will spend @ minimum of at least one full day per month with the intern at the local
school, and @ representative of the State Department of Education will spend a like
amount of time in the same manner. These supervision times should coincide or overlap sufficiently so as to facilitate at least one conference per month involving
representatives of the three agencies concerned.
All concerned shall agree to a plan for evaluation of the internship.
